task: Implemented macOS voice notification system for askr init to enable users to opt-in to spoken state updates via native `say` command, integrated with existing Discord notification hooks to announce task completion and session state transitions; fixed handover generation bug leaking .claude/ directory noise into uncommitted files list.
discussion_summary: Session focused on two parallel tracks: (1) completing voice notification preference schema implementation by adding load_voice_enabled() and save_voice_enabled() helper functions to askr/state/config.py following existing config pattern, and (2) investigating and fixing a real handover bug in checkpoint.py where _get_uncommitted_files() was dumping raw git status output without filtering, causing .claude/ directory artifacts to pollute the handover document. Confirmed no active nested worktree lockout condition exists currently; prior session's guard lockout was collision-specific, not a standing bug. Architecture for voice notifications remains: machine-level boolean flag in ~/.config/askr/config.json, wired through notification hooks as additional sink alongside Discord.

accomplishments.what: Scoped voice notification feature: confirmed macOS-only deployment enables native `say` TTS without cross-platform abstraction
Identified notification hook integration points (askr/hooks/stop.py, notification.py) for wiring voice updates as additional sink alongside Discord
Designed voice notification preference schema: machine-level boolean flag stored in global ~/.config/askr/config.json (not per-project)
Implemented load_voice_enabled() and save_voice_enabled() helper functions in askr/state/config.py following existing config pattern
Diagnosed handover generation bug: _get_uncommitted_files() in checkpoint.py dumping raw git status output without .claude/ filtering
Fixed checkpoint.py to exclude .claude/ directory from uncommitted files list in handover documents
Verified no active nested worktree lockout condition; prior session's guard collision was event-specific, not standing bug

in_progress.what: Architectural design for stateful retry mechanism that captures failure context (screenshots, error reasoning) to enable learning-based job resubmission instead of blind retry
Wire voice_enabled preference through askr init flow and integrate with existing notification hooks as additional sink alongside Discord

next_actions.action: Integrate voice_enabled preference into askr/hooks/stop.py notification flow to call native `say` command when voice_notifications is enabled
next_actions.why: load_voice_enabled() and save_voice_enabled() are implemented but not yet wired into the actual notification pipeline; voice feature is incomplete without this integration

next_actions.action: Add voice notification opt-in prompt to askr init flow (likely in askr/cli/init.py or equivalent) to capture user preference on first run
next_actions.why: Voice preference helpers exist but users have no way to set the preference yet; init flow must prompt for voice_notifications boolean

next_actions.action: Test voice notification end-to-end: run askr init with voice enabled, verify `say` command fires on task completion and state transitions
next_actions.why: Feature is architecturally complete but untested; need to confirm native macOS TTS integration works as designed

next_actions.action: Resume work on stateful retry mechanism design: define failure context schema (screenshots, error reasoning, job metadata) for learning-based resubmission
next_actions.why: Architectural design is in-progress; this is the next major feature after voice notifications and will improve job success rates

decisions.decision: Voice notifications are machine-level user trait, not per-project
decisions.reason: Mirrors existing pattern of developer name; users want consistent voice preference across all projects on a machine

decisions.decision: Voice notifications are macOS-only feature using native `say` command
decisions.reason: Avoids cross-platform TTS abstraction complexity; native command is reliable and requires no external dependencies

decisions.decision: Voice notifications hook into existing notification infrastructure as additional sink alongside Discord
decisions.reason: Minimal mechanical changes required; reuses established notification dispatch pattern in stop.py and notification.py

decisions.decision: Handover uncommitted files list must exclude .claude/ directory
decisions.reason: Claude session artifacts (.claude/) are internal tooling noise and pollute handover documents; they should never appear in project state

failed_approaches.approach: Dumping raw git status --short --porcelain output directly into handover uncommitted files list
failed_approaches.reason: Includes .claude/ directory artifacts and other internal tooling noise that pollutes handover documents and confuses future sessions

relational_files.why: Will integrate voice_enabled preference into notification pipeline; currently imports notification.py
Existing notification dispatch sink; voice notifications will be added as additional sink alongside Discord
Will add voice notification opt-in prompt to askr init flow to capture user preference on first run
Should add test coverage for load_voice_enabled() and save_voice_enabled() helper functions
